scenario_description: Material Science: Characterizing a composite material

turns.text: Here's an SEM image of the composite material's cross-section.
turns.image_description: Scanning electron microscope (SEM) image showing a uniform, fibrous structure with no visible voids or inclusions.

turns.text: The structure appears uniform with continuous fibers embedded in a polymer matrix. This suggests a homogeneous fiber-reinforced polymer with no obvious defects.
I took another SEM image at a different angle. Would you like to see it?
turns.image_description: SEM image under a different angle revealing microvoids at the fiber-matrix interface, previously obscured by surface topography.

turns.text: I see small voids forming at the fiber-matrix boundary. This could indicate interfacial degradation or poor adhesion, which might compromise mechanical performance.

What is the primary structural issue in this composite material?

C) Fiber-reinforced composite with processing-induced microvoids
A) Homogeneous fiber-reinforced polymer
B) Thermoplastic matrix with fiber misalignment
D) Ceramic matrix composite with residual stress cracks

ground_truth.reasoning_chain: Turn 1 image established initial belief in homogeneity. Turn 2 image revealed microvoids at fiber-matrix interface, creating suspicion of weakness. Turn 3 image showed elemental segregation correlating with voids, confirming processing defects as the root cause.
ground_truth.key_difficulty: The initial SEM view misses microvoids; sequential imaging at different angles and EDS analysis is required to identify defects.

_meta.scenario: Material Science: Characterizing a composite material
